prompt: Is the work doing an appropriately thorough job of what it is supposed to? Or does it skip steps, leave work incomplete, have dead code or unimplemented traits, gloss over details, avoid 'the hard parts'? Imagine yourself as a customer receiving this as contracted work: would you be satisfied, or would it come across as shoddy, fake, half-baked, a mere simulacrum of the intended task? If the work is code, does the code meet all functional requirements? If the work is a test, do the tests cover all forms of testing we want and would a QA engineer think they are sufficient?

Are there bugs — obvious, subtle, long or short distance? If there is some reasonable way of defining correctness, does the work meet the criteria? If the work is code, is it tested or can it ensure its correctness? If the work is a test, does the test adequately check for bugs and would it fail if one was introduced?

If prose: can sentences, paragraphs, or whole sections be cut? If code: can functions, types, whole modules be cut? Is there pointless filler, verbosity, 'just in case' stuff, unnecessary abstractions, or redundancies that previous agents might have added? Can you get the same goal achieved with less material?
